Another floor will be added ia
the new building of the Standard
Furniture Co. on the south side OF
Riverside avenue between Wash.
ington and Bernard streets, max.
ing 1t seven stories. The building
is 66k Fet wide by 1870 long, front
ing on both Riverside and
Sprague.


A 54o-acre ranch near Valley,
WASH, has been sold by H.
Weatherwax to George Bastian, re-
Gently from Iowa, for $4,000.


The city engineer has appoint
ed Mike ]. Heney as chief fore
man of construction on the Monroe
street bridge at salary of $40 a
day.


The Trustee Co. will expend $40,-
900 in remodeling the Eagle block.
The Raymond hotel is vacating the
building and the four upper floors
will be converted into modern Of
flee rooms, finished after the style
of the Hyde block, owned by the
same company. The Trustee Co.
paid $260.000 for the Eagle block
last March.


The report Of Horace kimbali,
receiver of the State Bank of
Washington. that failed in 1906,
has reported to court that collec-
tions amounting to $18,552.S9 have
been made, that in addition he has
recovered judgment that Is worth
from $4,000 to $4,000, and has other
probable assets that will enable
him to make fair settlement with
creditors. The liabilities are about
$4,000.


Professor e. c. Knapp, the Chi.
cago Sunday school organizer, has
transferred his activities for the
balance of this week to Walla
Walla, Columbia and North Yakima


Edward Folsom, shot by his wife
at Marshall Junction October s, is
recovering. The bullet still re-
poses in his chest. Mrs. Folsom is
in custody, awaiting the outcome
Of her husbands injuries.


The Northern Pacific will make
q general change in its passenger
schedules Sunday, October "a, at
which time ne wtranscontinental
train will be put into service.


An effort led by +. w. King and
others has been launched to eject
Karl Riedelsberger from the direc-
torship of the Spokane Choral so
ciety. The chief objection to the
leader is his action In retiring from
the program at the irrigation con
gress in sympathy with the
musicians union of this city.


The Great Northern will add
sleeping car service to the mail
flyer recently put on that line
which has cut the time between
st. Paul and Seattle Is hours. At
present the train carries mail only.


r. NJ. Barnhart, former prose-
cutting attorney, is confined to his
home with an injury to his knee
cap received while romping ON the
